2. `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` lists 23 untracked .ts files across src/runtime/ (9), src/harness/ (7: parity-gate, full-target-gate, full-target-verifier, runtime-gate, codex-lifecycle-exercise, m8-boundary-evidence, ui-agreement), src/events/ledger.ts, src/projection/ (2), src/memory/ (2), src/policy/permission-broker.ts, src/composition/composition.ts, src/runtime-architecture.test.ts, plus docs/architecture/PHYSICAL_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md.
3. `git log --all --oneline` for src/runtime/ src/harness/ src/events/ src/memory/ src/policy/ src/projection/ src/composition/ returns EMPTY — these trees were never committed in any ref, including the two agent/run worktree branches.
4. `git ls-tree -r origin/main` contains only the same 3 src files, so a fresh clone gets none of the runtime/harness/evidence machinery.
5. `git check-ignore -v` exits non-zero for the untracked files — they are NOT gitignored; this is plain uncommitted work, not intentional exclusion.
6. HEAD commit d3daaeb ('Prevent false completion claims with executable gates') touches only docs, scripts/live-integration-smoke.mjs, src/cli.ts, src/core.ts, src/core.test.ts — none of the runtime trees.

DECISIVE EVIDENCE the finding understates: the committed code directly depends on the untracked code. src/cli.ts:7-12 imports from ./events/ledger.js, ./harness/full-target-gate.js, ./harness/m8-boundary-evidence.js, ./harness/codex-lifecycle-exercise.js, ./harness/ui-agreement.js, ./harness/full-target-verifier.js. src/core.ts:6-13 imports from ./events/ledger.js, ./projection/projection.js, ./projection/sqlite-store.js, ./composition/composition.js, ./runtime/codex-process-bridge.js, ./policy/permission-broker.js, ./runtime/omx-adapter.js, ./runtime/agy-adapter.js. Therefore a fresh clone of origin/main has tracked cli.ts/core.ts importing 14 symbols from files that do not exist in the repo — it cannot compile (tsc) or run. The '95 tests pass / build clean' claim and the 7-hard-gates-PASS / 95-completion-ceiling narrative in HARD_COMPLETION_GATES.md and FULL_PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md depend entirely on local-only ephemeral files. Milestone docs (CURRENT_BASELINE_GAP_REPORT.md, FULL_PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md, DOGFOOD_REPORT.md) reference these modules by name. The 'durable evidence' product ships its central evidence machinery as untracked, unrecoverable-from-remote files.

findings.title: Committed HEAD and working tree are two divergent products; the 'real' product is uncommitted
findings.evidence: git show HEAD:src/core.ts has NO cross-tree imports (grep exit 1) and is 477 lines; the working-tree src/core.ts is 506 lines and at lines 6-13 imports from ./events/ledger.js, ./projection/projection.js, ./projection/sqlite-store.js, ./composition/composition.js, ./runtime/codex-process-bridge.js, ./policy/permission-broker.js, ./runtime/omx-adapter.js, ./runtime/agy-adapter.js. Same split in cli.ts (HEAD 91 lines/no cross-tree imports; working tree 110 lines importing ./events/ledger.js and 5 ./harness/* modules at lines 7-12). A pristine 'git worktree add HEAD' checkout builds clean and runs 62 tests pass -- but it is the OLD flat engine, not the advertised runtime product. The advertised product (working tree) depends on 13+ untracked files and cannot be reconstructed from git at all.
findings.claim_vs_reality: The repo presents itself as a single coherent delivered product. In fact the committed/pushed code is a self-contained earlier engine, while the runtime-architecture product everyone is reviewing exists only as modified-but-uncommitted core.ts/cli.ts plus untracked dependencies. Cloning the repo and running it would give a different, smaller program than the one demonstrated.
findings.recommendation: Commit the working-tree core.ts/cli.ts together with all the untracked modules they import in a SINGLE atomic commit so HEAD compiles. Verify atomicity by building a fresh 'git worktree add' of the new HEAD before claiming anything -- I demonstrated the current HEAD worktree contains only 3 src files.

verdict.reasoning:
Every factual claim independently reproduced.

LINE COUNTS / IMPORTS: `git show HEAD:src/core.ts | wc -l` = 477; working tree src/core.ts = 506. HEAD cli.ts = 91; working tree = 110 (matches finding exactly). `git show HEAD:src/core.ts | grep` for cross-tree imports → exit 1 (none); same for HEAD cli.ts → exit 1. Working tree src/core.ts:6-13 imports ./events/ledger.js, ./projection/projection.js, ./projection/sqlite-store.js, ./composition/composition.js, ./runtime/codex-process-bridge.js, ./policy/permission-broker.js, ./runtime/omx-adapter.js, ./runtime/agy-adapter.js. Working tree src/cli.ts:7-12 imports ./events/ledger.js + 5 ./harness/* modules. All confirmed verbatim.

UNTRACKED DEPS: `git ls-files` across src/events src/projection src/composition src/runtime src/policy src/memory src/harness docs/architecture = 0 tracked files. `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` = 25 untracked files including every imported module. Per-file `git ls-files`/`git check-ignore` shows each dependency is tracked=[] AND ignored=[] — genuinely never committed, not git-ignored. .gitignore only covers node_modules/dist/build/local-state. src/core.ts and src/cli.ts both show ` M` (modified vs HEAD); diff HEAD = 68 insertions across the two files.

HEAD CHECKOUT IS THE OLD FLAT ENGINE: `git worktree add --detach HEAD` produced src/ containing ONLY cli.ts, core.test.ts, core.ts — no events/runtime/harness/etc dirs. `npm run build` → exit 0; `node --test` → tests 62, pass 62, fail 0. Exactly matches the finding's "builds clean and runs 62 tests pass" claim.

ADVERTISED PRODUCT UNRECONSTRUCTABLE FROM GIT: Overlaid working-tree core.ts/cli.ts onto a clean HEAD checkout (which contains everything git tracks) and ran tsc → 14 `TS2307: Cannot find module` errors, one per cross-tree import (./events/ledger.js, ./projection/*, ./composition/*, ./runtime/codex-process-bridge.js, ./policy/*, ./runtime/omx-adapter.js, ./runtime/agy-adapter.js, all 6 cli harness modules). The runtime-architecture product cannot compile from committed state.

CONCLUSION: The committed/pushed HEAD is a self-contained 477-line flat engine (62 tests). The advertised runtime-architecture product exists only as uncommitted modifications to core.ts/cli.ts plus 25 untracked dependency files. A `git clone` yields a different, smaller program than the one under review. This is a genuine process-hygiene/integrity defect of the highest order: the entity being reviewed and certified (roadmap matrices, hard-gate PASS claims) is not the entity in version control.

Minor calibration note on the finding's phrasing: the orchestrator summary said "95 tests pass" for the working tree while HEAD yields 62 — consistent with two divergent products (the extra ~33 tests live in the untracked src/runtime-architecture.test.ts and harness suites). This strengthens rather than weakens the finding.

findings.title: The '95 tests pass' verified-fact depends on untracked files and is non-deterministic (flaky)
findings.evidence: Pristine HEAD worktree (committed-only) runs node --test on dist/core.test.js => tests 62, pass 62. The untracked src/runtime-architecture.test.ts contains 33 test() declarations. 62 committed + 33 untracked = 95, so the '95 tests' figure is only reachable with untracked files present. Running the full working-tree suite 7 times gave: 95/0, 94/1, 93/2, 95/0, 94/1, 94/1, 94/1 fail. Failing tests rotate among 'product gate rejects forged manual independent review artifacts', 'product gate durable report contains report_path after hard gates pass', and 'hard completion ceiling requires independent review and reconciliation artifacts'. The assertion at dist/core.test.js:848 is AssertionError actual 'PASS', expected 'FAIL' -- the anti-self-deception gate test intermittently sees PASS where it must see FAIL.
findings.claim_vs_reality: Commit messages assert 'Tested: npm test (95)' / '52/52' / '46/46' as a clean reproducible gate, and HARD_COMPLETION_GATES.md claims all 7 gates PASS. Reality: the gate cannot pass from committed sources alone, and even with untracked files the suite is flaky -- the hard-completion gate itself sometimes fails by returning PASS when the test demands FAIL, i.e. the anti-inflation gate inflates.
findings.recommendation: Make the suite deterministic before trusting any gate: isolate per-test temp dirs/state and avoid shared mutable fixtures across the parallel node:test runner (the failing tests share product-gate state and run for 1.5-2.8s each). Re-baseline test counts only after the runtime test file is committed. Never cite a flaky suite as a completion gate.

verdict.reasoning: Independently verified every load-bearing claim. (1) 95 requires untracked files: `git archive HEAD | tar -x` yields ONLY src/core.test.ts (no runtime/harness/events/memory/policy/projection/composition dirs exist in HEAD); building+running committed-only gives exactly `tests 62`. `src/runtime-architecture.test.ts` is `??` untracked (git status --short confirms) with 33 test() declarations. 62+33=95 — the "95 tests" verified-fact is unreachable from committed sources. (2) Flakiness confirmed: 8 working-tree runs gave 94/1, 95/0, 95/0, 95/0, 94/1, 94/1, 93/2, 94/1; failing tests rotate among the gate tests at src/core.test.ts:924 (`hard completion ceiling requires independent review and reconciliation artifacts`), :956 (`product gate durable report contains report_path after hard gates pass`), and :579 (`product gate rejects metadata-complete review forgery without notification envelope`). (3) The anti-inflation gate inflates: the AssertionError observed is `'FAIL' !== 'PASS'` at the runProductGate assertion (src/core.test.ts:929/:932/:960; compiled verbatim in dist/core.test.js), proving the completion gate is non-deterministic. Root cause (strengthens finding): the flaky tests run against process.cwd() (the REAL repo) not an isolated mkdtempSync dir — they rmSync/rewrite .agent/independent-review-gate.json in the live tree (file is currently left behind at /Users/dominic/Documents/github/dominic_orchestration/.agent/independent-review-gate.json), creating order/history-dependent shared state. Even at --test-concurrency=1 run 1 failed (94/1), so it is genuine shared-state non-determinism, not merely a parallel-file race. The committed-only suite is itself failing 60/62 consistently. Nuance: I more often saw the assertion direction `expected PASS got FAIL` rather than the finding's `expected FAIL got PASS`, but both directions equally prove the anti-self-deception gate is flaky; substance fully holds. HARD_COMPLETION_GATES.md's 'all 7 gates PASS' and the commit-message 'Tested: npm test (95)' claims are not reproducible from the committed tree and are flaky even with untracked files.

findings.title: Milestone evidence docs and the live-smoke gate are committed while their substrate is not
findings.evidence: git status shows 4 milestone docs modified-but-uncommitted (CURRENT_BASELINE_GAP_REPORT.md, DOGFOOD_REPORT.md, FULL_PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md, HARD_COMPLETION_GATES.md). scripts/live-integration-smoke.mjs IS committed (added in d3daaeb) and shells out to dist/cli.js, but dist/ is gitignored (.gitignore line: dist/) and built from working-tree (untracked-dependent) sources -- so the smoke a reviewer runs exercises code that is not in git. .agent/ durable artifact store has 0 tracked files (the PRD's central durable evidence store is untracked/ignored). docs/architecture/PHYSICAL_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md (the architecture of record) is untracked.
findings.claim_vs_reality: DOGFOOD_REPORT / PRODUCT_GATE_RERUN_REPORT / HARD_COMPLETION_GATES present committed durable evidence of a gated product. Reality: the docs are partly uncommitted, the architecture doc is untracked, the smoke runs against an out-of-VCS dist built from out-of-VCS sources, and the durable artifact store the PRD centers on is not versioned. The evidence chain is not reproducible from the repository.
findings.recommendation: Commit the modified milestone docs and docs/architecture/ in the same change as the code they describe. Either commit a build provenance hash or have the smoke build from a clean checkout so evidence is reproducible. Decide explicitly whether .agent/ sample artifacts belong in VCS as fixtures; if the gate depends on them, they must be tracked or generated deterministically.

verdict.reasoning:
Every concrete claim is independently verified, and the central thesis ("evidence chain not reproducible from the repository") holds.

1. Four milestone docs modified-but-uncommitted: git status shows `M docs/milestones/{CURRENT_BASELINE_GAP_REPORT,DOGFOOD_REPORT,FULL_PRODUCT_ROADMAP,HARD_COMPLETION_GATES}.md`.

2. scripts/live-integration-smoke.mjs is committed (in d3daaeb; git ls-files confirms tracked) and at lines 16 and 18 spawns join(root,'dist','cli.js'); it also reads .agent/runs/... (lines 37,39,44) and writes its own evidence to .agent/live-integration-smoke.json (scripts/live-integration-smoke.mjs:50).

3. dist/ is gitignored: .gitignore:13 `dist/`; `git check-ignore dist/cli.js` matches. So the smoke runs a binary never in VCS.

4. .agent/ is gitignored (.gitignore:3 `.agent/`) and `git ls-files .agent/` returns 0 — the PRD-central durable artifact store is unversioned. The working-tree .agent/live-integration-smoke.json exists but is ignored.

5. docs/architecture/PHYSICAL_RUNTIME_ARCHITECTURE.md is the only file in docs/architecture/ and is untracked (0 tracked files there).

6. "dist built from out-of-VCS sources" — verified and sharper than stated. Cloned HEAD to /tmp: src/ contains ONLY cli.ts, core.ts, core.test.ts; runtime/harness/events/projection/memory/policy/composition trees ABSENT (24 untracked src files via `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard src/`). Committed HEAD cli.ts/core.ts do NOT import those trees, so a clone builds and `node dist/cli.js init` runs (EXIT=0). The untracked-tree imports (./events/ledger.js, ./harness/*, ./projection/*, ./runtime/*, ./policy/*, ./composition/*) exist ONLY in the uncommitted working-tree modifications: src/cli.ts:7-12 and src/core.ts:6-13 (git diff --stat: cli.ts +25, core.ts +63). Thus the gate-passing product the docs certify = modified-uncommitted cli.ts/core.ts + 24 untracked source files + gitignored dist/ + gitignored .agent/. None reproducible from git; a reviewer who clones gets a different, smaller product than HARD_COMPLETION_GATES.md:27 ("Live Integration Gate ... PASS") certifies.

The finding's CLAIM_VS_REALITY is accurate. Minor nuance: the finding implied HEAD's cli.ts already depends on untracked trees; in fact that dependency lives in the uncommitted working-tree diff — which strengthens the conclusion, since even the developer's own evidence was produced against an out-of-VCS working state.

findings.title: Stray orchestrator worker branches and worktrees left in the workspace
findings.evidence: git branch -a shows agent/run-20260601063424374-asui12-task-20260601063424260-8/worker-001 and .../worker-002. git worktree list shows two live worktrees under /Users/dominic/Documents/github/dominic_orchestration.agent-worktrees/run-20260601063424374-.../worker-001 and worker-002, both checked out at d3daaeb. These are orchestrator-generated artifacts left over after a run, sitting beside the repo.
findings.claim_vs_reality: The product claims clean bounded multi-worker lifecycle management. Reality: a prior multi-worker run left orphaned branches and worktrees in place with no cleanup, accumulating VCS cruft.
findings.recommendation: Add lifecycle cleanup that removes per-worker worktrees and branches (or prunes them on run completion/cancellation), and exclude the sibling .agent-worktrees directory by convention. Verify no worker branch is mistaken for delivered work.

verdict.reasoning: low severity, not independently verified
